name: Coconut-Lime Pie
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Coconut cream, not coconut milk
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the piecrust and allow it to cool completely.
2. For the filling, sprinkle water over gelatin in a cup and let stand at least 2 minutes to soften.
3. In a quart microwave-safe measuring cup or bowl, whisk cornstarch and 1/4 cup milk until smooth. Whisk in egg yolks, 1 teaspoon lime zest, and salt. Whisk in the remaining milk and coconut cream until blended.
4. Microwave on high for 3 minutes. Whisk until smooth. Continue microwaving for 3 to 7.5 more minutes, whisking every 2 minutes, until boiling, thickened, and smooth. Stir in softened gelatin until it dissolves.
5. Pour the mixture into a medium-sized bowl (not aluminum) and stir in the remaining lime zest and lime juice.
6. Place the bowl in a larger bowl half-filled with ice water. Stir the mixture often until cool and thick enough to form soft mounds. Stir in vanilla. Remove from the ice-water bath.
7. Beat cream with an electric mixer until soft peaks form. Gently stir into the filling.
8. Pour the filling into the piecrust. Cover with plastic wrap (to prevent a skin from forming) and refrigerate at least 2 hours, or up to 3 days, until set.
9. For topping: Beat heavy cream, coconut cream, and vanilla in a medium-size bowl until stiff peaks form. Pipe a lattice design on the filling, then a border around the edge of the pie (using a pastry bag fitted with a star tip) or spread the cream on the filling. Sprinkle with toasted coconut if desired. Refrigerate until ready to serve.
